commands.build: opa eval -d policy-bundle/ -d control-state.json "data.policy.report.compliance_report" --format pretty > compliance-report.json

manual_preconditions: The OPA binary (1.18.2 or later) must be on PATH or present in the repository root. No other runtime, package manager, or network access is required.
control-state.json must be the assigned pack copy, verified against the staff signature before use.
Commands are given in POSIX form. The verified environment was Windows 11 with PowerShell; the PowerShell equivalents are documented in README.md.
known_limitations: The acceptance suite contains 41 assertions, which include the 18 published fixtures plus positive, malformed-input, exception-expiry, audit-log, and violation-precedence cases. Passing it does not prove correctness against the hidden state fixtures.

Log-sink validation applies the full existence-and-immutability check only when the logging inventory is present in loaded data. Standalone fixture inputs, which carry no inventory, are accepted on a non-empty sink reference.
The written analysis covers only the three assigned control outcomes. Other identified gaps are dispositioned as reduce, defer, or accept in control-mapping.csv rather than implemented.
Runtime and memory figures were recorded on a single machine and are indicative, not a performance guarantee.
